learning_objective: After solving this problem, the student will be able to implement breadth-first search on an unweighted graph to find the shortest path (minimum number of edges) between two nodes, and correctly handle edge cases such as disconnected graphs, self-loops, and cycles.
prerequisites: queue data structure and FIFO operations
story_direction: A network of N computers is connected by M bidirectional communication links. A message must be sent from computer 1 to computer N, relaying through intermediate computers. Each link takes one hop to traverse. The goal is to find the minimum number of hops needed to deliver the message from computer 1 to computer N.
